problem_title: Bicycle Shop Database Schema with SQLAlchemy
question: Write code to create a database schema for an online bicycle shop that sells bicycles as well as their spare parts and accessories. Use SQAlchemy library to communicate with the database. The code should cover opening and closing of a new connection to the database, creation of necessary tables and their relations, common operations on items in the tables such as addition, deletion, updates, filtered selection and search.

paraphraser.0.9: Author code to fashion a database schema supporting an online cycle store that peddles bikes including their additional components and attachments. 0.0:
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    parts = relationship('BicyclePart', backref='bicycle')
    accessories = relationship('Accessory', backref='bicycle')

class BicyclePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///bicycle_shop.db')
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Add a new bicycle
bicycle = Bicycle(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', price=500)
session.add(bicycle)
session.commit()

# Add a new bicycle part
part = BicyclePart(name='Wheel', price=50, bicycle=bicycle)
session.add(part)
session.commit()

# Add a new accessory
accessory = Accessory(name='Helmet', price=30, bicycle=bicycle)
session.add(accessory)
session.commit()

# Update a bicycle's price
bicycle.price = 550
session.commit()

# Delete a bicycle part
session.delete(part)
session.commit()

# Filter and select bicycles by brand
filtered_bicycles = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(brand='Trek').all()

# Search for a bicycle by model
searched_bicycle = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(model='Mountain Bike').first()

session.close()

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    accessories = relationship('Accessory', backref='bicycle')
    parts = relationship('Part', backref='bicycle')

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///bicycle_shop.db')
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Add a new bicycle
new_bicycle = Bicycle(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', price=500)
session.add(new_bicycle)
session.commit()

# Add a new accessory
new_accessory = Accessory(name='Helmet', price=50, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_accessory)
session.commit()

# Add a new part
new_part = Part(name='Tire', price=20, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_part)
session.commit()

# Update a bicycle
bicycle_to_update = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(id=new_bicycle.id).first()
bicycle_to_update.price = 550
session.commit()

# Delete an accessory
accessory_to_delete = session.query(Accessory).filter_by(id=new_accessory.id).first()
session.delete(accessory_to_delete)
session.commit()

# Select bicycles with a certain brand
bicycles_by_brand = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(brand='Trek').all()
for bicycle in bicycles_by_brand:
    print(bicycle.model, bicycle.brand, bicycle.price)

# Search for a bicycle by model
bicycle_by_model = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(model='Mountain Bike').first()
if bicycle_by_model:
    print(bicycle_by_model.model, bicycle_by_model.brand, bicycle_by_model.price)
else:
    print('Bicycle not found')

session.close()

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

# Create the database engine
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///bicycle_shop.db')

# Create the base class for the ORM models
Base = declarative_base()

# Define the Bicycle model
class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    parts = relationship('Part', backref='bicycle')
    accessories = relationship('Accessory', backref='bicycle')

# Define the Part model
class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

# Define the Accessory model
class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

# Create the tables in the database
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

# Open a new connection to the database
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Add a new bicycle
new_bicycle = Bicycle(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', price=799)
session.add(new_bicycle)
session.commit()

# Add a new part
new_part = Part(name='Derailleur', price=50, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_part)
session.commit()

# Add a new accessory
new_accessory = Accessory(name='Bike Helmet', price=35, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_accessory)
session.commit()

# Update the price of a bicycle
bicycle = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(id=new_bicycle.id).first()
bicycle.price = 849
session.commit()

# Delete a part
part = session.query(Part).filter_by(id=new_part.id).first()
session.delete(part)
session.commit()

# Select all bicycles
all_bicycles = session.query(Bicycle).all()
for bicycle in all_bicycles:
    print(f'Model: {bicycle.model}, Brand: {bicycle.brand}, Price: {bicycle.price}')

# Select all parts for a specific bicycle
bicycle_parts = session.query(Part).filter_by(bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id).all()
for part in bicycle_parts:
    print(f'Part Name: {part.name}, Price: {part.price}')

# Close the database connection
session.close()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey, Float
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, backref
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    parts = relationship('Part', backref='bicycle')
    accessories = relationship('Accessory', backref='bicycle')

class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

engine = sqlalchemy.create_engine('sqlite:///bicycle_shop.db')
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

def add_bicycle(model, brand, price):
    bicycle = Bicycle(model=model, brand=brand, price=price)
    session.add(bicycle)
    session.commit()
    return bicycle

def add_part(name, price, bicycle):
    part = Part(name=name, price=price, bicycle=bicycle)
    session.add(part)
    session.commit()
    return part

def add_accessory(name, price, bicycle):
    accessory = Accessory(name=name, price=price, bicycle=bicycle)
    session.add(accessory)
    session.commit()
    return accessory

def get_bicycles():
    return session.query(Bicycle).all()

def get_parts(bicycle):
    return session.query(Part).filter_by(bicycle=bicycle).all()

def get_accessories(bicycle):
    return session.query(Accessory).filter_by(bicycle=bicycle).all()

def update_bicycle(bicycle, model, brand, price):
    bicycle.model = model
    bicycle.brand = brand
    bicycle.price = price
    session.commit()

def delete_bicycle(bicycle):
    session.delete(bicycle)
    session.commit()

def search_bicycles(model=None, brand=None, min_price=None, max_price=None):
    query = session.query(Bicycle)
    if model:
        query = query.filter_by(model=model)
    if brand:
        query = query.filter_by(brand=brand)
    if min_price:
        query = query.filter(Bicycle.price >= min_price)
    if max_price:
        query = query.filter(Bicycle.price <= max_price)
    return query.all()

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey, DateTime
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

# Create a connection to the database
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///bicycle_shop.db')
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)

# Declare the Base class for the ORM
Base = declarative_base()

# Define the Bicycle model
class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    in_stock = Column(Integer)
    parts = relationship('Part', backref='bicycle')
    accessories = relationship('Accessory', backref='bicycle')

# Define the Part model
class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    in_stock = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

# Define the Accessory model
class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    in_stock = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

# Create the database tables
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

# Open a new session
session = Session()

# Add a new bicycle
new_bicycle = Bicycle(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', category='Mountain', price=599, in_stock=10)
session.add(new_bicycle)
session.commit()

# Add a new part
new_part = Part(name='Suspension Fork', description='High-performance suspension fork', price=199, in_stock=20, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_part)
session.commit()

# Add a new accessory
new_accessory = Accessory(name='Bike Helmet', description='Safety helmet for cycling', price=49, in_stock=50, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_accessory)
session.commit()

# Retrieve all bicycles
all_bicycles = session.query(Bicycle).all()
for bicycle in all_bicycles:
    print(f"Model: {bicycle.model}, Brand: {bicycle.brand}, Category: {bicycle.category}, Price: {bicycle.price}, In Stock: {bicycle.in_stock}")

# Retrieve all parts for a specific bicycle
bicycle_parts = session.query(Part).filter_by(bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id).all()
for part in bicycle_parts:
    print(f"Part: {part.name}, Description: {part.description}, Price: {part.price}, In Stock: {part.in_stock}")

# Retrieve all accessories for a specific bicycle
bicycle_accessories = session.query(Accessory).filter_by(bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id).all()
for accessory in bicycle_accessories:
    print(f"Accessory: {accessory.name}, Description: {accessory.description}, Price: {accessory.price}, In Stock: {accessory.in_stock}")

# Update the price of a bicycle
bicycle = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(id=new_bicycle.id).first()
bicycle.price = 649
session.commit()

# Delete a part
part = session.query(Part).filter_by(id=new_part.id).first()
session.delete(part)
session.commit()

# Close the session
session.close()

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey, Float
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    inventory = Column(Integer)
    components = relationship('Component', backref='bicycle')
    addons = relationship('Addon', backref='bicycle')

class Component(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'components'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

class Addon(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'addons'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

def open_database_connection(db_url):
    engine = create_engine(db_url)
    Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
    session = Session()
    return session

def close_database_connection(session):
    session.close()

def add_bicycle(session, model, brand, price, inventory):
    bicycle = Bicycle(model=model, brand=brand, price=price, inventory=inventory)
    session.add(bicycle)
    session.commit()
    return bicycle

def add_component(session, name, price, bicycle):
    component = Component(name=name, price=price, bicycle=bicycle)
    session.add(component)
    session.commit()
    return component

def add_addon(session, name, price, bicycle):
    addon = Addon(name=name, price=price, bicycle=bicycle)
    session.add(addon)
    session.commit()
    return addon

def delete_bicycle(session, bicycle):
    session.delete(bicycle)
    session.commit()

def update_bicycle(session, bicycle, model, brand, price, inventory):
    bicycle.model = model
    bicycle.brand = brand
    bicycle.price = price
    bicycle.inventory = inventory
    session.commit()

def get_bicycles(session, **filters):
    return session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(**filters).all()

def search_bicycles(session, query):
    return session.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.model.like(f'%{query}%') | Bicycle.brand.like(f'%{query}%')).all()

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey, Float
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship

# Create the database engine
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///bike_store.db')

# Create the declarative base
Base = declarative_base()

# Define the Bicycle table
class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    category = Column(String)
    components = relationship('Component', backref='bicycle')
    addons = relationship('Addon', backref='bicycle')

# Define the Component table
class Component(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'components'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

# Define the Addon table
class Addon(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'addons'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

# Create the tables in the database
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

# Create a session
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Add a new bicycle
new_bicycle = Bicycle(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', price=599.99, category='Mountain')
session.add(new_bicycle)
session.commit()

# Add a new component
new_component = Component(name='Suspension Fork', price=199.99, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_component)
session.commit()

# Add a new addon
new_addon = Addon(name='Bike Rack', price=49.99, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_addon)
session.commit()

# Query the database
bicycles = session.query(Bicycle).all()
for bicycle in bicycles:
    print(f'Model: {bicycle.model}, Brand: {bicycle.brand}, Price: {bicycle.price}')
    for component in bicycle.components:
        print(f'- Component: {component.name}, Price: {component.price}')
    for addon in bicycle.addons:
        print(f'- Addon: {addon.name}, Price: {addon.price}')

# Close the session
session.close()

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    type = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    components = relationship('Component', backref='bicycle')
    add_ons = relationship('AddOn', backref='bicycle')

class Component(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'components'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    type = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

class AddOn(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'add_ons'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///bike_store.db')
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Adding a new bicycle
new_bicycle = Bicycle(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', type='Mountain', price=999)
session.add(new_bicycle)
session.commit()

# Adding a new component
new_component = Component(name='Suspension Fork', type='Fork', price=249, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_component)
session.commit()

# Adding a new add-on
new_add_on = AddOn(name='Bike Lock', price=59, bicycle_id=new_bicycle.id)
session.add(new_add_on)
session.commit()

# Deleting a bicycle
session.delete(new_bicycle)
session.commit()

# Modifying a component
component = session.query(Component).filter_by(name='Suspension Fork').first()
component.price = 279
session.commit()

# Selecting bicycles with a filter
mountain_bikes = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(type='Mountain').all()

# Searching for a bicycle by model
bike = session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(model='Mountain Bike').first()

session.close()

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

# Create the database engine
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///cycle_store.db')

# Create the base class for database models
Base = declarative_base()

# Define the Bike model
class Bike(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bikes'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)

    components = relationship('Component', backref='bike')
    attachments = relationship('Attachment', backref='bike')

# Define the Component model
class Component(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'components'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))

# Define the Attachment model
class Attachment(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'attachments'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))

# Create the database tables
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

# Create a session for interacting with the database
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Add a new bike
bike = Bike(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', price=1000)

# Add components to the bike
component1 = Component(name='Suspension Fork', price=300, bike=bike)
component2 = Component(name='Rear Derailleur', price=200, bike=bike)

session.add(component2)

# Add attachments to the bike
attachment1 = Attachment(name='Bike Rack', price=50, bike=bike)
attachment2 = Attachment(name='Fenders', price=30, bike=bike)
session.add(attachment1)
session.add(attachment2)

# Commit the changes to the database
session.commit()

# Query the database
bikes = session.query(Bike).all()
for bike in bikes:
    print(f"Model: {bike.model}, Brand: {bike.brand}, Price: {bike.price}")
    print("Components:")
    for component in bike.components:
        print(f"- {component.name}, Price: {component.price}")
    print("Attachments:")
    for attachment in bike.attachments:
        print(f"- {attachment.name}, Price: {attachment.price}")

from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey, DateTime
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bike(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bikes'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    year = Column(Integer)
    frame_size = Column(String)
    color = Column(String)

    components = relationship('Component', backref='bike')
    attachments = relationship('Attachment', backref='bike')

class Component(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'components'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    type = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))

class Attachment(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'attachments'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    type = Column(String)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))

class InventoryRecord(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'inventory_records'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))
    component_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('components.id'))
    attachment_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('attachments.id'))
    action = Column(String)
    timestamp = Column(DateTime)

from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///cycle_store.db')

session = Session()

# Example usage
bike = Bike(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', year=2021, frame_size='M', color='Red')

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

# Create the database engine
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///cycle_store.db')

# Create the base class for declarative models
Base = declarative_base()

# Define the Bike model
class Bike(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bikes'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    components = relationship('Component', backref='bike')

# Define the Component model
class Component(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'components'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))
    attachments = relationship('Attachment', backref='component')

# Define the Attachment model
class Attachment(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'attachments'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    component_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('components.id'))

# Create the database tables
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

# Create a session for interacting with the database
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Add a new bike
bike = Bike(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', price=1000)
session.add(bike)

# Add components to the bike
component1 = Component(name='Wheels', price=200, bike=bike)
component2 = Component(name='Handlebars', price=100, bike=bike)
session.add_all([component1, component2])

# Add attachments to the components
attachment1 = Attachment(name='Tire', price=50, component=component1)
attachment2 = Attachment(name='Grip', price=20, component=component2)
session.add_all([attachment1, attachment2])

# Commit the changes to the database
session.commit()

# Query the database
bikes = session.query(Bike).all()
for bike in bikes:
    print(f"Model: {bike.model}, Brand: {bike.brand}, Price: {bike.price}")
    for component in bike.components:
        print(f"  Component: {component.name}, Price: {component.price}")
        for attachment in component.attachments:
            print(f"    Attachment: {attachment.name}, Price: {attachment.price}")

# Close the session
session.close()

from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey, DateTime, Numeric
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bike(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bikes'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Numeric)
    components = relationship('Component', backref='bike')

class Component(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'components'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Numeric)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))
    attachments = relationship('Attachment', backref='component')

class Attachment(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'attachments'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    price = Column(Numeric)
    component_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('components.id'))

class Transaction(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'transactions'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))
    component_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('components.id'))
    attachment_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('attachments.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    total_price = Column(Numeric)
    timestamp = Column(DateTime)

from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker

engine = create_engine('sqlite:///cycle_store.db')
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Add a new bike
bike = Bike(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', price=999.99)
session.add(bike)

# Add a new component
component = Component(name='Suspension Fork', price=249.99, bike=bike)
session.add(component)

# Add a new attachment
attachment = Attachment(name='Bike Light', price=29.99, component=component)
session.add(attachment)

# Create a new transaction
transaction = Transaction(bike=bike, component=component, attachment=attachment, quantity=1, total_price=1279.97, timestamp='2023-04-24 12:34:56')
session.add(transaction)

session.commit()

# Query the database
bikes = session.query(Bike).all()
for bike in bikes:
    print(f'Model: {bike.model}, Brand: {bike.brand}, Price: {bike.price}')
    for component in bike.components:
        print(f'    Component: {component.name}, Price: {component.price}')
        for attachment in component.attachments:
            print(f'        Attachment: {attachment.name}, Price: {attachment.price}')

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

# Create the database engine
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///cycle_store.db')

# Create the base class for ORM models
Base = declarative_base()

# Define the Bike model
class Bike(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bikes'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    model = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    color = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)

    components = relationship('Component', backref='bike')
    attachments = relationship('Attachment', backref='bike')

# Define the Component model
class Component(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'components'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))

# Define the Attachment model
class Attachment(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'attachments'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Integer)
    bike_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bikes.id'))

# Create the database tables
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

# Create a session
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Add a new bike
bike = Bike(model='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', color='Red', price=599)
session.add(bike)

# Add components to the bike
component1 = Component(name='Suspension Fork', description='High-quality suspension fork', price=199, bike=bike)
component2 = Component(name='Rear Derailleur', description='Smooth gear shifting', price=79, bike=bike)
session.add(component1)
session.add(component2)

# Add attachments to the bike
attachment1 = Attachment(name='Bike Rack', description='Sturdy bike rack', price=49, bike=bike)
attachment2 = Attachment(name='Fenders', description='Protect from mud and water', price=29, bike=bike)
session.add(attachment1)
session.add(attachment2)

# Commit the changes
session.commit()

# Query the database
bikes = session.query(Bike).all()
for bike in bikes:
    print(f'Model: {bike.model}, Brand: {bike.brand}, Color: {bike.color}, Price: {bike.price}')
    print('Components:')
    for component in bike.components:
        print(f'- {component.name} ({component.description}), Price: {component.price}')
    print('Attachments:')
    for attachment in bike.attachments:
        print(f'- {attachment.name} ({attachment.description}), Price: {attachment.price}')
